sn21.3:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn21.3:1.3: And on that occasion Ven. Sāriputta & Ven. Mahā Moggallāna were staying near Rājagaha in a single dwelling in the Squirrels’ Sanctuary.
sn21.3:1.4: Then Ven. Sāriputta, arising from his seclusion in the late afternoon, went to Ven. Mahā Moggallāna. On arrival, he exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn21.3:1.5: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to Ven. Mahā Moggallāna,
sn21.3:2.2: pure your complexion, and clear. Could it be that Ven. Mahā Moggallāna has spent today in a peaceful abiding?”
sn21.3:2.3: “It was in a gross abiding, my friend, that I spent today.
sn21.3:2.4: But I had some Dhamma talk.”
sn21.3:2.5: “With whom did Ven. Moggallāna have some Dhamma talk?”
sn21.3:2.7: “But far away is the Blessed One now, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn21.3:2.8: Did Ven. Mahā Moggallāna go to the Blessed One through psychic power,
sn21.3:2.9: or did the Blessed One come to Ven. Mahā Moggallāna through psychic power?”
sn21.3:2.10: “I didn’t go to the Blessed One through psychic power, my friend,
sn21.3:2.11: nor did the Blessed One come to me through psychic power.
sn21.3:2.12: Simply that the Blessed One purified his divine eye & divine ear as far as me,
sn21.3:2.13: and I purified my divine eye & divine ear as far as the Blessed One.”
sn21.3:2.14: “And what kind of Dhamma talk did Ven. Mahā Moggallāna have with the Blessed One?”
sn21.3:3.1: “Just now, my friend, I said to the Blessed One,
sn21.3:3.2: ‘One of aroused persistence, one of aroused persistence,’ it is said, lord.
sn21.3:3.3: To what extent is a person one of aroused persistence?’
sn21.3:3.4: When this was said, the Blessed One said,
sn21.3:3.5: ‘There is the case, Moggallāna, where a monk, (thinking,)
sn21.3:3.6: “Gladly would I let the flesh & blood in my body dry up, leaving just the skin, tendons, & bones, but if I have not attained what can be reached through manly firmness, manly persistence, manly striving, there will be no relaxing my persistence”:
sn21.3:3.7: That is how one is a person of aroused persistence.’
sn21.3:3.8: That is the Dhamma talk I had with the Blessed One, my friend.”
sn21.3:4.1: “Friend, like a few small pieces of gravel placed next to the Himalayas, the king of mountains,
sn21.3:4.2: are we when placed next to Ven. Mahā Moggallāna,
sn21.3:4.3: for Ven. Mahā Moggallāna is of such great power, great might, that if he wished he could live for an eon.”
sn21.3:5.1: “Friend, like a few small grains of salt placed next to a large salt barrel
sn21.3:5.2: are we when placed next to Ven. Sāriputta,
sn21.3:5.3: for in many ways has Ven. Sāriputta been lauded, praised, & extolled by the Blessed One:
sn21.3:6.2: Any monk who has gone beyond,
sn21.3:6.3: at best can only equal him
sn21.3:7.1: In this way did each of these two great beings [nāga] approve of what was well-stated and well-expressed by the other.
